title: Notogramme japonica
intro: Notogramme japonica, also known as Japanese Notogramme, is a type of fern plant belonging to the family Lindsaeaceae. Notogramme japonica has been used in traditional medicine to exhibit various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Notogramme japonica is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Notogramme japonica is classified within the family Polypodiaceae and order Polypodiales, which places it among the ferns.
